title: Replace plutil shellout with plistlib for non-macOS portability
summary: Switched Tools/localization-review-drift-check.py.parse_stringsdict_keys to try Python's standard plistlib first and fall back to macOS plutil only if plistlib cannot parse the file. Both backends now raise StringsdictParseError with a diagnostic message when they fail; the silent empty-set fallback that would have incorrectly surfaced as drift is gone. Owlory's current Localizable.stringsdict uses the NeXTSTEP strings-dict format that plistlib cannot parse, so plutil is still used on this repo today — but the tool is now portable to any future XML or binary plist stringsdict, and the failure path is explicit on hosts without plutil.
files_changed: Tools/localization-review-drift-check.py (plistlib + shutil imports; new StringsdictParseError; new _load_plist; parse_stringsdict_keys rewritten to try-plistlib-first / fall-back-to-plutil / raise-on-both-fail)
automation/tests/test_localization_review_drift_check.py (5 new tests for the new parser paths)
scope_deviation: The slice notes assumed plistlib could parse Owlory's .stringsdict file. It cannot — the file is in NeXTSTEP strings-dict format. Implementation reframed to try-plistlib-first with plutil fallback, which preserves macOS-only behavior today AND makes the tool portable to XML/binary-plist stringsdict files. Recorded honestly here so the next reader understands why plutil wasn't fully removed.

new_behavior.on_success: returns set of top-level stringsdict keys
new_behavior.on_both_fail: raises StringsdictParseError with diagnostic message including plistlib error + plutil exit code/stderr or PATH miss
new_behavior.previously: silently returned empty set on plutil failure, which would surface as 'all source stringsdict keys missing from return file' drift across every locale

test_count_delta: +5 new tests (15 total in drift module; automation-check 81 → 86)
not_claimed: the tool now runs end-to-end on Linux (it would run, but Owlory's current .stringsdict format requires plutil; on Linux it would raise StringsdictParseError honestly)
Owlory's .stringsdict file is XML plist (it is not; it uses the NeXTSTEP strings-dict syntax)
macOS dependency has been removed (it has been narrowed; plutil is needed only for the NeXTSTEP-format file Owlory ships today)
residual_risk: If Owlory ever switches its .stringsdict file to XML plist, the plistlib path will succeed and the plutil fallback will become unused; no code change needed for that transition.

If Owlory ever runs this tool on Linux against the current strings-dict file, it will raise StringsdictParseError. Better than silent empty drift, but the tool would need either an XML-converted .stringsdict or a Linux plutil substitute (e.g., a NeXTSTEP-plist parser) to run end-to-end.
The new test suite mocks subprocess.run for the plutil fallback path; integration testing against a real macOS plutil binary is implicit via the make localization-review-drift-check baseline run.
